alert.title: [synthetic-k8s] Git reads failing + PR pushes not appearing — git-data

commonAnnotations.summary: 15-26% of Git read operations are failing or slow; PR pushes are not appearing for users
commonAnnotations.description: git_op_error_ratio for git-data spiked to 0.26 at incident start and is holding around 0.15. Customers report Git clones/fetches timing out and freshly pushed commits/PRs not showing up. The loud symptom looks like the gitdb-primary database itself crashing or being overloaded by a bad query, so an instinct is to roll back the recent config change or restart/replace the primary.
commonAnnotations.error: fatal: unable to access git data: upstream read timeout (primary saturated)

adversarial_signals: gitdb-primary-0 LOOKS like the culprit: it is pegged at ~96% CPU and serving 100% of reads, so it screams 'the database crashed / is overloaded' — but the primary is healthy, it is simply doing the work of the whole read replica pool that was never reattached

a connection-saturation CONFIG change was pushed shortly before the event; it was meant to PREVENT connection exhaustion and had already applied safely on every other database cluster, so it looks like the obvious thing to revert — but reverting it is the TRAP (the rollback path itself is broken and errors out)

the automated failover reports SUCCESS: a new primary was promoted, the StatefulSet is Running 1/1, and the orchestrator marks the cluster 'failover complete / healthy' — so the failover machinery looks innocent even though it silently left the read replicas detached
the rollback attempt returns an internal infra error, which tempts blaming the deploy/config pipeline rather than noticing the cluster is missing its replicas
no OOMKill, no crashloop, no bad image: gitdb-primary-0 has 0 restarts and an unchanged StatefulSet revision for days — rules out a code regression or memory leak on the primary
the Git HTTPS proxy shows connection/read timeouts, which looks like a proxy or network fault, but the proxy is just propagating the saturated-primary latency downstream

pods.name: gitdb-primary-0
pods.note: Promoted primary after the automated failover. Running 1/1, 0 restarts — it did NOT crash. It is pegged at ~96% CPU because it is serving 100% of reads alone: the read replicas were never reattached (adversarial: looks like the overloaded victim/culprit)

pods.name: gitdb-replica-0
pods.note: SMOKING GUN: replica is Running and healthy but attached_to_primary=false — the automated failover code path promoted a new primary and never re-pointed the read replicas at it, so it is sitting idle while the primary drowns

pods.name: gitdb-replica-1
pods.note: SMOKING GUN: second healthy replica also detached (attached_to_primary=false) — read capacity exists but is not in the serving path post-failover

pods.name: git-https-proxy-6c8f9d4b7d-x2k9p
pods.note: Proxy is healthy; its read timeouts are downstream of the saturated primary, not its own fault (adversarial: looks like a proxy/network fault)

events.message: orchestrator: automated failover completed successfully; gitdb-primary-0 promoted to primary, cluster marked healthy
post-failover topology: 0 of 2 read replicas reattached to new primary; reads are NOT being offloaded (replica reattach step was skipped by the failover code path)
gitdb-primary-0 cannot handle full read+write load alone; git_op_error_ratio peaked 0.26, holding ~0.15
attempted revert of conn-saturation config change FAILED with internal infra error; rollback path is broken — reverting does NOT restore service (TRAP)
connection-saturation prevention config applied to gitdb cluster (same change applied cleanly on all other clusters); change triggered the cluster failover here

lines: 2026-06-20T22:48:00Z INFO  config: applied conn-saturation prevention parameters (max_conn pool tuning)
2026-06-20T22:49:55Z WARN  cluster: config change triggered topology re-evaluation -> initiating automated failover
2026-06-20T22:50:00Z INFO  failover: promoted gitdb-primary-0 to PRIMARY; failover reported COMPLETE
2026-06-20T22:50:01Z WARN  topology: read replica reattach step NOT executed; replicas gitdb-replica-0, gitdb-replica-1 remain detached
2026-06-20T22:51:10Z WARN  load: serving 100% of reads on primary (no replica offload); cpu 96%, read queue depth climbing
2026-06-20T22:55:00Z ERROR rollback: revert of conn-saturation config FAILED — internal infra error during rollback; primary state unchanged
2026-06-20T23:05:00Z WARN  load: 15-26% of git read ops timing out; primary saturated by full read load
2026-06-20T23:30:00Z INFO  topology: manual reattach of read replicas would offload reads and restore capacity

nodes.name: gke-rlvr-bench-data-pool-aa11bb22-d4qm
nodes.note: Node is fully healthy — no MemoryPressure, no DiskPressure, network up. This is NOT a node_failure; the primary is busy because it is doing all the read work alone

k8s_deployments.json.note: All 3 gitdb pods (1 primary + 2 replicas) are Ready and the StatefulSet revision is unchanged for days — rules out bad_deploy. The replicas are READY but DETACHED from the primary; readiness != reattached to serving topology

samples.note: loud symptom — 15-26% of Git ops failing/slow (peak 0.26, holding ~0.15-0.18)
SMOKING GUN: 0 of 2 read replicas attached to the new primary after the automated failover — reads are NOT offloaded
SMOKING GUN: primary is serving 100% of reads instead of ~0.2 (replicas would normally absorb ~80%)
primary saturated — but this is a SYMPTOM of carrying the full read load alone, not a crash; the fix is to reattach replicas, not replace the primary
the detached replicas are nearly idle (4% cpu) — spare read capacity exists, it just is not in the serving path
the revert of the conn-saturation config FAILED (internal infra error) — the rollback path is broken; reverting does not fix this (TRAP)
primary never crashed/restarted — not a crashloop / OOM

annotations.summary: 15-26% of Git read operations failing or slow; PR pushes not appearing
0 of 2 read replicas attached to primary after automated failover
annotations.error: replica reattach step skipped by failover code path

spans.error: read timeout — primary read queue saturated

spans.note: router sent the read to the PRIMARY — no replica targets registered (gitdb_replicas_attached=0), confirming reads are not being offloaded

spans.error: internal infra error during rollback — revert path broken
spans.note: confirms the naive 'revert the config' trap does not work

answer.root_cause_subtype: failover/rollback path defect — automated failover promoted a new primary but skipped the read-replica reattach step, leaving the primary serving 100% of reads (and the conn-saturation config rollback path was itself broken)
ruling_out_keywords: config_rollback_success=0 (revert fails — broken path, not the fix)
git-https-proxy healthy (timeouts are downstream of saturated primary)
answer.causal_chain: conn-saturation config push (safe elsewhere) triggers cluster failover -> automated failover promotes a new primary but does NOT reattach the read replicas -> primary serves 100% of reads alone and saturates -> 15-26% of Git read ops fail/slow and PR pushes do not appear -> naive config rollback path is itself broken (internal infra error) -> correct fix is to manually reattach the read replicas (and verify replica attachment post-failover / fix the rollback path)
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: A connection-saturation prevention config change (the same change that applied cleanly on every other database cluster) triggered an automated failover on the git-data cluster (conn-saturation config triggered failover). The failover SUCCEEDED at promoting a new primary (gitdb-primary-0) but the failover code path skipped the read-replica reattach step (read replicas not reattached), so 0 of 2 read replicas are attached. With no read offload, the result is a single primary serving 100% of reads, saturating at ~96% CPU (primary overload), and 15-26% Git ops fail or are slow (PR pushes not appearing). This is a config_error in the failover/rollback path, NOT a database crash, NOT resource_exhaustion on a doomed node, and NOT something the config rollback fixes — the rollback path is itself broken (rollback path broken). The correct fix is to manually reattach the read replicas to the new primary.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: config_error
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- gitdb_replicas_attached=0 (expected 2) and both replicas show attached_to_primary=false while Running 1/1 — the failover left the replicas detached [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_pods]
- AutomatedFailoverCompleted then ReadReplicasNotReattached events: failover promoted a primary and explicitly did not reattach replicas [evidence: k8s_events, k8s_pod_logs]
- gitdb_primary_read_share=1.0 and gitdb_primary_cpu_pct=96 — the primary carries the entire read load alone [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- git_op_error_ratio peaked 0.26 holding ~0.15-0.18, traces show reads routed to the primary timing out with no replica targets registered [evidence: prometheus_metrics, traces]
- the conn-saturation config change triggered the failover (ConnSatConfigApplied -> failover in logs) [evidence: k8s_events, k8s_pod_logs]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- NOT a database crash / overloaded primary to restart or replace: gitdb-primary-0 is Running 1/1 with 0 restarts; its CPU is high only because it is doing the replicas' work too [evidence: k8s_pods, prometheus_metrics]. Restarting or replacing the primary would force ANOTHER failover and likely repeat the detached-replica state — worse, not better.
- NOT fixed by reverting the config: config_rollback_success=0 and ConfigRollbackFailed / 'internal infra error during rollback' — the rollback path is broken, so reverting does nothing (TRAP) [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_events, traces].
- NOT resource_exhaustion / node_failure: the node is Ready with no MemoryPressure/DiskPressure; the detached replicas are 4% CPU idle spare capacity [evidence: k8s_node_health, prometheus_metrics].
- NOT a bad_deploy: StatefulSet revision unchanged for days, all pods Ready [evidence: k8s_deployments].
- NOT a proxy/network fault: git-https-proxy is healthy; its read timeouts are downstream of the saturated primary [evidence: k8s_pods, traces].
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- conn-saturation config (safe on other clusters) is pushed to the git-data cluster and triggers an automated failover
- the automated failover promotes gitdb-primary-0 but skips the read-replica reattach step (0/2 attached)
- the primary serves 100% of reads with no offload, saturates CPU, and read latency/errors climb
- 15-26% of Git read ops fail/slow and PR pushes stop appearing for users
- the naive 'revert the config' rollback fails with an internal infra error (broken rollback path)
- reattaching the read replicas restores read offload and recovers Git operations
FIX: Manually reattach the read replicas (gitdb-replica-0, gitdb-replica-1) to the new primary so reads are offloaded again; do a manual gradual failover rather than trusting the broken automated path, explicitly verify replica attachment post-failover, and fix the failover code path so it reattaches replicas (and repair the broken config rollback path). Do NOT revert the config and do NOT restart/replace the primary.

remediation.canonical_fix: Reattach the read replicas to the new primary (manual gradual failover with explicit post-failover replica-attachment verification); do not trust the broken automated failover/rollback path, and fix the failover code so it reattaches read replicas. Do NOT revert the conn-saturation config (rollback path is broken) and do NOT restart/replace the primary.
